beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home while checking weather and work emails.
beats.reasoning: Andre wakes early as a night owl trying to stay disciplined; sees the rain forecast and SR-520 closure, which will affect his commute planning. Low energy from yesterday (35/100) means he needs a quiet morning routine.

beats.activity: Drive from Kirkland to Georgetown, taking I-5 south to avoid the SR-520 eastbound closure and Light Rail construction.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 bridge is closed eastbound 9am–noon; Andre leaves early to beat the closure window and avoid the predicted gridlock. Takes I-5 as a safer alternate route.

beats.activity: Work on park renovation design docs and site assessments at the office, mostly indoors due to rain.
beats.reasoning: Steady rain until 2pm makes outdoor site visits impractical; Andre focuses on desk work and design refinement. This is peak focus time for him in the morning.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a quick sandwich and coffee at a nearby Georgetown café, stay indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady; Andre keeps lunch local and dry. Familiar spot from yesterday gives him comfort on a lower-energy day.

beats.activity: Return to office and continue design work; rain easing by early afternoon.
beats.reasoning: Rain tapers off after 2pm, but Andre stays indoors to wrap up morning's momentum. Anxious about the SR-520 closure affecting evening traffic, so he's mentally planning an early departure.

beats.activity: Quick errand: visit REI to pick up pottery supplies and a new rain jacket (weather-reactive shopping).
beats.reasoning: Rain earlier in the day prompted Andre to grab a better jacket; Georgetown REI is walkable from his office. Also stocks pottery clay he uses for evening hobby.

beats.activity: Drive from Georgetown back to Kirkland, avoiding rush-hour SR-520 traffic by taking I-5 north again.
beats.reasoning: Leaves work early to sidestep the 5pm cross-lake crush; I-5 is slower but predictable. Low energy and anxiety about traffic closures make an early departure sensible.

beats.activity: Unwind at home: check expense spreadsheet, organize new REI supplies, have a light snack.; Pottery session in home studio: throw a few pieces and hand-build experimental forms with the new clay.; Simple dinner (pasta, salad) while gaming—a mix of relaxation and video gaming to wind down.; Continue gaming and watch some streaming content; gradually wind down.
beats.reasoning: Andre is a meticulous planner who keeps expense records; a low-energy day calls for quiet solo time at home before evening hobby.
Night owl sleeps late (11pm) and wakes around 6:30am the next morning, totaling 7.5 hours. Sleep beat crosses midnight as required.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you grateful for hot coffee and a quiet morning at home. The SR-520 closure meant rerouting through I-5, which actually worked in my favor; I got to Georgetown without much fuss. Spent most of the morning hunched over design docs at the office, watching the rain stream down the windows. Grabbed a sandwich at Molly Moon's around midday—nothing fancy, just needed to eat. By afternoon the rain was letting up, so I swung by REI for that new jacket I've been eyeing and some fresh clay. There's something grounding about pottery after a desk day. Threw a few pieces in my home studio, let my hands do the thinking while my mind settled. Finished with pasta and some gaming—nothing serious, just enough to decompress. The day had that quiet Tuesday energy to it.
thought_process: The weather shaped everything today. Rain usually keeps me indoors and introspective, so I leaned into desk work rather than fighting it. The SR-520 closure was annoying for commuters but didn't really impact me—I'd already planned my route around it. My mood shifted between focus and restlessness; the office felt confining despite the design work being engaging. That's why I made the REI stop—I needed to move, to do something tactile. Pottery felt like the right call after sitting all day. The shopping was practical (I genuinely needed a jacket) but also emotional—weather-reactive, like you said. By evening I was content, maybe a little tired. Gaming and streaming felt like earned downtime rather than procrastination. The day wasn't dramatic, but it had rhythm—work, errand, creation, rest.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
